stem: A degree-\( [[n]] \) Taylor polynomial about \( a = [[a]] \) has Lagrange remainder \( R_{[[n]]}(x) = \dfrac{f^{([[n]]+1)}(\xi)}{([[n]]+1)!}(x-a)^{[[n]]+1} \). Given \( \left|f^{([[n]]+1)}(\xi)\right| \le [[M]] \) for all \( \xi \) between \( a \) and \( x \), find the resulting upper bound on \( \left|R_{[[n]]}([[x]])\right| \).
distractor_rationales: This uses order \( [[n]] \) throughout (\( |x-a|^{[[n]]}/[[n]]! \)) instead of \( [[n]]+1 \).
This forgets to increment the factorial to \( ([[n]]+1)! \), leaving \( [[n]]! \).
This replaces the factorial \( ([[n]]+1)! \) with just \( [[n]]+1 \).
This omits the bound \( M = [[M]] \) on the derivative.
This is off by one from the correct bound.
This is off by one from the correct bound.

stem: The Maclaurin polynomial of degree \( [[n]] \) for \( f(x) = [[fn]] \) is used to approximate \( f([[x]]) \). Every derivative of \( [[fn]] \) satisfies \( \left|f^{(k)}(\xi)\right| \le 1 \) for all \( \xi \), so the Lagrange remainder satisfies \( \left|R_{[[n]]}(x)\right| \le \dfrac{|x|^{[[n]]+1}}{([[n]]+1)!} \). Find this upper bound at \( x = [[x]] \).
solution: Since \( M=1 \) bounds every derivative of \( [[fn]] \), the bound is \( \dfrac{|[[x]]|^{[[n]]+1}}{([[n]]+1)!} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses order \( [[n]] \) throughout instead of \( [[n]]+1 \).
This forgets to increment the factorial to \( ([[n]]+1)! \), leaving \( [[n]]! \).
This replaces the factorial \( ([[n]]+1)! \) with just \( [[n]]+1 \).
This drops the absolute value, which can give a negative bound for odd powers.
This is off by one from the correct bound.
This is off by one from the correct bound.

stem: A degree-\( [[n]] \) Taylor polynomial \( P_{[[n]]}(x) \) about \( a = [[a]] \) approximates a function \( f \), with \( P_{[[n]]}([[x]]) = [[Pval]] \). The Lagrange remainder satisfies \( \left|R_{[[n]]}([[x]])\right| \le [[M]]\cdot\dfrac{|[[x]]-[[a]]|^{[[n]]+1}}{([[n]]+1)!} \). Since \( f([[x]]) = P_{[[n]]}([[x]]) + R_{[[n]]}([[x]]) \), what is the largest possible value of \( f([[x]]) \)?
solution: The largest possible value occurs when the remainder attains its maximum positive value: \( f([[x]]) \le P_{[[n]]}([[x]]) + \dfrac{M|[[x]]-([[a]])|^{[[n]]+1}}{([[n]]+1)!} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This subtracts the remainder bound instead of adding it, giving the smallest possible value instead of the largest.
This reports the remainder bound itself, forgetting to add the approximation \( P_{[[n]]}([[x]]) \).
This multiplies the approximation by a factor involving the bound instead of adding the bound.
This uses order \( [[n]] \) throughout instead of \( [[n]]+1 \) in the remainder bound.
This is off by one from the correct maximum value.
This is off by one from the correct maximum value.
